Name: MFA not configured for privileged accounts
Description:
This indicator checks that MFA (Multi-factor Authentication) is enabled for users assigned privileged roles.

Required permissions

  • RoleManagement.Read.Directory
  • Reports.Read.All
LikelihoodOfCompromise:
Users that hold privileged roles in Entra ID are high-value targets for an attacker. It is critical for organizations to require MFA for all users holding privileged roles.

The risk associated with compromise of a privileged user is equal to the permissions and rights held within the role assigned to the user.

Implementing changes to Conditional Access policies and enabling Security Defaults can negatively impact users if not properly tested.

References

Secure your Microsoft Entra identity infrastructure - Microsoft Entra ID | Microsoft Learn

Require MFA for administrators with Conditional Access - Microsoft Entra ID | Microsoft Learn

Providing a default level of security in Microsoft Entra ID - Microsoft Entra | Microsoft Learn
ResultMessage: {0} privileged user(s) found without MFA configured.
Remediation:
Users identified as impacted must be configured to require MFA at minimum for authentication in Entra ID.

Break-glass accounts may be an exception to MFA requirements, and can be added to an ignore list for this indicator. Organizations need to monitor their break-glass accounts to ensure that the accounts are not used without awareness

Microsoft provides both a Conditional Access policy template, as well as step-by-step instructions for creating a Conditional Access Policy requiring MFA for administrators. If a Conditional Access policy already exists for privileged users, the identified users can be added to that policy.

Organizations that do not have licensing for Conditional Access policies should enable Security Defaults.
